advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Merge or cross-link the new 'Disk-full triage' section with the pre-existing 'Disk full — snapshot-leak recovery' section so there is one authoritative root-disk triage entry.
evidence: infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:304: `## Disk full — snapshot-leak recovery (incident 2731 / bug 9d7c)`
Both sections reference the same `rebar-root-disk-pressure` alarm, `/tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots`, `docker builder prune`, and `/rerun` recovery.
infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:353: ``rebar-root-disk-pressure` alarm clears once `df` drops back under 85%.`
advisory.finding: The new 'Disk-full triage (bug 7a41)' section (line 354) substantially duplicates the pre-existing 'Disk full — snapshot-leak recovery (incident 2731 / bug 9d7c)' section (line 304): both cover the same rebar-root-disk-pressure alarm, the same snapshot store, docker builder/image prune, and /rerun recovery. Two overlapping triage sections invite operator confusion and content drift.
merged_from: The new H2 heading is not preceded by a blank line — line 353 (prose) is immediately followed by line 354 ('## Disk-full triage ...'). Many markdown linters (markdownlint MD022) require blank lines around headings, and the missing separation hurts readability of the rendered doc.
advisory.suggested_fix: Fold the new triage content into the existing incident-2731 disk-full section (or add an explicit cross-reference) rather than maintaining two parallel sections.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the pre-existing disk-full section so it no longer claims the review-bot clones into the rebar-gate-snapshots store; the voter uses a reviewbot- TemporaryDirectory.
evidence: infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:308: `the content-addressed snapshot store on the **ROOT** disk (/tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots)`
infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:390: `The voter clones each change into /tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="reviewbot-")/`
src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py:283: `def clone_change_ref(self, change_number: int, revision_ref: str, dest: str) -> str:` — clones into dest, not the snapshot store
advisory.finding: The new section correctly documents that the review-bot voter clones into `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="reviewbot-")`, but the pre-existing section (line 308-309) states 'Every review clones the change into the content-addressed snapshot store (/tmp/rebar-gate-snapshots)'. These are two distinct mechanisms: voter.py clones via clone_change_ref into a reviewbot- tmpdir, which is separate from the rebar-gate-snapshots store. The change adds a correct account without reconciling the now-contradictory older text.
